title: Command `podman system df` returns a negative reclaimable size of images
body:
### Issue Description

Command `podman system df` returns a negative reclaimable size of images.

```
[web@s4 ~]$ podman system df
TYPE           TOTAL       ACTIVE      SIZE        RECLAIMABLE
Images         8           6           1.311GB     -5.989e+08B (-46%)
Containers     15          15          196.2kB     0B (0%)
Local Volumes  0           0           0B          0B (0%)
```

It happens after I run 2 containers based on custom images with a common ancestor image, and then run `podman image prune -a`.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

```bash
# Start from clean Alma Linux 9.
sudo -i
dnf update
dnf install podman

podman --version
#podman version 4.9.4-rhel

mkdir test12 test13

for t in test12 test13; do
  cat <<EOF >"${t}/Dockerfile"
FROM docker.io/library/busybox:latest
RUN echo "${t}" >${t}.txt
CMD ["sh", "-c", "while true; do cat ${t}.txt; sleep 5; done"]
EOF
  podman build --tag "${t}:latest" "${t}"
  podman run -d "${t}"
done

podman system df
#TYPE           TOTAL       ACTIVE      SIZE        RECLAIMABLE
#Images         5           2           4.539MB     4.538MB (100%)
#Containers     2           2           22.68kB     0B (0%)
#Local Volumes  0           0           0B          0B (0%)

podman image prune -a
#WARNING! This command removes all images without at least one container associated with them.
#Are you sure you want to continue? [y/N] y
#27a71e19c95622dce4d60d4a3760707495c9875f5c5322c5bd535214799593ce
#ff85c5105a9ccd2209e7892bc827377002769cf5f8578ebb45672a5cb166a643
#ae7c431f01c373313df85b278eda9a7c068e909737d113da37d91158ab80e193

podman system df
#TYPE           TOTAL       ACTIVE      SIZE        RECLAIMABLE
#Images         2           2           4.522MB     -4.506e+06B (-100%)
#Containers     2           2           22.68kB     0B (0%)
#Local Volumes  0           0           0B          0B (0%)
```

<details>
<summary>My original steps</summary>

`php-base` is a custom image based on PHP. 
`mybb-custom` and `grav-custom` are custom images based on `php-base`.

```

[web@s4 ~]$ podman --version
podman version 4.9.4-rhel
```

</details>

### Describe the results you received

Unexpected value in Images-RECLAIMABLE.
```
[web@s4 ~]$ podman system df
TYPE           TOTAL       ACTIVE      SIZE        RECLAIMABLE
Images         8           6           1.311GB     -5.989e+08B (-46%)
Containers     15          15          196.2kB     0B (0%)
Local Volumes  0           0           0B          0B (0%)
```

### Describe the results you expected

No reclaimable space after pruning.
```
[web@s4 ~]$ podman system df
TYPE           TOTAL       ACTIVE      SIZE        RECLAIMABLE
Images         8           6           1.311GB     0B (0%)
Containers     15          15          196.2kB     0B (0%)
Local Volumes  0           0           0B          0B (0%)
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Root or rootless - issue reproduced in both cases.

### Upstream Latest Release

Reproduced in both: 
* `4.9.4-rhel` latest in the Alma Linux 9 repositories
* Current `main` as of 2024-11-13

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
Do you have an exact reproducer? How are the images being created?

I know this has been reported before elsewhere but without knowing the exact steps on how to get into this situation there is little we can do.

Also we only support the latest release upstream. As such it would be good if you can try with the latest version if you are unable to provide an exact reproducer
I see, the latest version is 5.2 and I'm using 4.9 (the latest in the default repos for Alma Linux).

@Luap99 this is the simplest I can go. Looks like the point is that there are 2 containers running based on 2 images that share a common ancestor image.

I'll update the OP with these steps in a moment. 

```bash
# Start from clean Alma Linux 9.
dnf update
dnf install podman

podman --version
#podman version 4.9.4-rhel

mkdir test12 test13

for t in test12 test13; do
  cat <<EOF >"${t}/Dockerfile"
FROM docker.io/library/busybox:latest
RUN echo "${t}" >${t}.txt
CMD ["sh", "-c", "while true; do cat ${t}.txt; sleep 5; done"]
EOF
  podman build --tag "${t}:latest" "${t}"
  podman run -d "${t}"
done

podman system df
#TYPE           TOTAL       ACTIVE      SIZE        RECLAIMABLE
#Images         5           2           4.539MB     4.538MB (100%)
#Containers     2           2           22.68kB     0B (0%)
#Local Volumes  0           0           0B          0B (0%)

podman image prune -a
#WARNING! This command removes all images without at least one container associated with them.
#Are you sure you want to continue? [y/N] y
#27a71e19c95622dce4d60d4a3760707495c9875f5c5322c5bd535214799593ce
#ff85c5105a9ccd2209e7892bc827377002769cf5f8578ebb45672a5cb166a643
#ae7c431f01c373313df85b278eda9a7c068e909737d113da37d91158ab80e193

podman system df
#TYPE           TOTAL       ACTIVE      SIZE        RECLAIMABLE
#Images         2           2           4.522MB     -4.506e+06B (-100%)
#Containers     2           2           22.68kB     0B (0%)
#Local Volumes  0           0           0B          0B (0%)
```
Thank you, this is more than simple enough and reproduces for me using podman from main so it was not fixed in the meantime.
Just adding the RHEL Jira card that this is being tracked in: https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-29641
